2026-04-21

The project is on GitHub: https://github.com/ChrisW12372/2026-04-21_p8106-hw04.git

library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(party)
library(partykit)
library(randomForest)
library(ranger)
library(gbm)
library(xgboost)

set.seed(202604)

1 Question One

df_college <- read.csv("data/College.csv", row.names = 1) |>
  janitor::clean_names()

1.1 Part I

1.1.1 Fit the Model

set.seed(202604)
data_split <- initial_split(df_college, prop = 0.8)

df_col_train <- training(data_split)
df_col_test <- testing(data_split)
set.seed(202604)
tree1 <- rpart(
  outstate ~ ., data = df_col_train,
  control = rpart.control(cp = 0.005)
)

cpTable1 <- tree1$cptable
print(cpTable1)
##             CP nsplit rel error    xerror       xstd
## 1  0.513231601      0 1.0000000 1.0043483 0.05694259
## 2  0.095506855      1 0.4867684 0.5015094 0.03258904
## 3  0.044930031      2 0.3912615 0.4446780 0.03182371
## 4  0.036275441      3 0.3463315 0.4462888 0.03547997
## 5  0.023244525      4 0.3100561 0.4267958 0.03462264
## 6  0.016225674      5 0.2868115 0.4037921 0.03161259
## 7  0.015980179      6 0.2705859 0.3961102 0.03157220
## 8  0.013732407      7 0.2546057 0.3920722 0.03168310
## 9  0.009061868      8 0.2408733 0.3749114 0.03181175
## 10 0.008871188      9 0.2318114 0.3708588 0.03078982
## 11 0.008293686     10 0.2229402 0.3655429 0.03059469
## 12 0.005774182     11 0.2146465 0.3705458 0.03109923
## 13 0.005723490     12 0.2088724 0.3630799 0.03083692
## 14 0.005435412     13 0.2031489 0.3641175 0.03089899
## 15 0.005065134     15 0.1922781 0.3636800 0.03088295
## 16 0.005000000     16 0.1872129 0.3654078 0.03091312

1.1.2 xerror

set.seed(202604)
min_err1 <- which.min(cpTable1[, "xerror"])
tree2 <- prune(tree1, cp = cpTable1[min_err1, "CP"])
rpart.plot(tree2)

1.1.3 1se rule

set.seed(202604)
min_row <- which.min(cpTable1[, "xerror"])
xerror_min <- cpTable1[min_row, "xerror"]
xstd_min   <- cpTable1[min_row, "xstd"]

threshold_1se <- xerror_min + xstd_min
candidate_rows <- which(cpTable1[, "xerror"] <= threshold_1se)
row_1se <- candidate_rows[1]

cp_1se <- cpTable1[row_1se, "CP"]

tree_1se <- prune(tree1, cp = cp_1se)

rpart.plot(tree_1se)

I randomly split the data into a training set (80%) and a test set (20%). Using the training data, I first fit a relatively large regression tree with an initial complexity parameter of 0.005. I then examined the cp table and considered two pruning strategies: selecting the cp that minimized the cross-validation error, and selecting the cp based on the 1-SE rule. These two approaches produced trees with different levels of complexity. Since the 1-SE tree was simpler and easier to interpret, I chose it as the final regression tree. The resulting plot is shown below.

1.2 Part II

1.2.1 Fit the Model

set.seed(202604)
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)
rf_grid <- expand.grid(mtry = 1:(ncol(df_col_train) - 1), 
                       splitrule = "variance", 
                       min.node.size = 1:6)

fit_rf <- train(outstate ~ ., 
                data = df_col_train, 
                method = "ranger", 
                tuneGrid = rf_grid,
                trControl = ctrl, 
                importance = "permutation")

1.2.2 Variable Importance

set.seed(202604)
fit_rf_final <- ranger(
  outstate ~ .,
  data = df_col_train,
  mtry = fit_rf$bestTune[[1]],
  splitrule = "variance",
  min.node.size = fit_rf$bestTune[[3]],
  importance = "permutation",
  scale.permutation.importance = TRUE
)

barplot(
  sort(ranger::importance(fit_rf_final), decreasing = FALSE),
  las = 2,
  horiz = TRUE,
  cex.names = 0.7,
  col = colorRampPalette(colors = c("cyan", "blue"))(length(ranger::importance(fit_rf_final)))
)

1.2.3 Test Error

rf_pred <- predict(fit_rf_final, data = df_col_test)$predictions
test_mse <- mean((rf_pred - df_col_test$outstate)^2)
test_rmse <- sqrt(test_mse)
test_mse
## [1] 3509856
test_rmse
## [1] 1873.461

  • The variable importance plot from the random forest model shows that “expend” is the most important predictor of “Outstate”, followed by “room_board”. Other influential variables include “accept, apps, terminal, and grad_rate”. In contrast, variables such as “books” and “s_f_ratio” appear to contribute relatively little to prediction accuracy.

  • Using the final random forest model, the test mean squared error (MSE) is 3,509,856, corresponding to a test root mean squared error (RMSE) of 1,873.461.

1.3 Part III

gbm_grid <- expand.grid(
  n.trees = c(100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000),
  interaction.depth = 1:4,
  shrinkage = c(0.005, 0.01, 0.05),
  n.minobsinnode = c(5, 10)
)
set.seed(202604)

fit_gbm <- train(
  outstate ~ .,
  data = df_col_train,
  method = "gbm",
  distribution = "gaussian",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneGrid = gbm_grid,
  verbose = FALSE
)

ggplot(fit_gbm, highlight = TRUE)
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the caret package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/topepo/caret/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

fit_gbm$bestTune
##     n.trees interaction.depth shrinkage n.minobsinnode
## 134     200                 4      0.05              5
gbm_imp <- summary(fit_gbm$finalModel, plotit = FALSE)
gbm_imp
##                     var   rel.inf
## expend           expend 50.752589
## room_board   room_board 10.818381
## terminal       terminal  5.885538
## perc_alumni perc_alumni  4.202443
## grad_rate     grad_rate  4.115682
## apps               apps  3.874135
## personal       personal  3.216638
## top10perc     top10perc  3.062111
## accept           accept  2.729788
## p_undergrad p_undergrad  2.285932
## f_undergrad f_undergrad  1.885289
## s_f_ratio     s_f_ratio  1.803650
## ph_d               ph_d  1.729103
## top25perc     top25perc  1.329975
## books             books  1.225856
## enroll           enroll  1.082891
barplot(
  sort(gbm_imp$rel.inf, decreasing = FALSE),
  names.arg = gbm_imp$var[order(gbm_imp$rel.inf)],
  las = 2,
  horiz = TRUE,
  cex.names = 0.7,
  col = colorRampPalette(colors = c("cyan", "blue"))(nrow(gbm_imp))
)

1.3.1 Test Error

gbm_pred <- predict(fit_gbm, newdata = df_col_test)

test_mse <- mean((gbm_pred - df_col_test$outstate)^2)
test_rmse <- sqrt(test_mse)

test_mse
## [1] 3576395
test_rmse
## [1] 1891.136
